summary: Crisp 1985 soundboard captures a ferocious OKC show highlighted by scorching Bertha, Stagger Lee and a hot Minglewood with Brent’s scat.
review: The September 2‑'85 Oklahoma City concert is an all‑out monster: the band bursts out of the gate with a blistering Bertha and never eases up, delivering fiery renditions of Stagger Lee, Minglewood (complete with Brent’s legendary scat), Me & My Uncle and a soaring second set featuring Playin’, China Doll and Bucket.  The soundboard source is clear, well‑balanced and captures the energy despite a brief Phil‑bass speaker hiccup, making this both a musical and technical high point of the ’85 tour.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews (15/19) award 4‑5 stars, praising both performance and soundboard quality; only a few mixed or negative comments mention minor speaker issues, yielding an overall strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, crisp mix with excellent instrument separation; occasional bass‑speaker overdrive on Phil’s amp and a brief bass trouble noted, but overall very listenable and well balanced.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set opens with a thunderous Bertha that sets a relentless pace, maintains momentum through hot mid‑set numbers like Stagger Lee and Minglewood, and caps the night with an adventurous second set featuring Playin', China Doll and Bucket – a consistently high‑energy flow from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring high notes, scorching guitar tone on Bertha and Stagger Lee, and overall hot playing throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Strong bass work highlighted on Me & My Uncle; noted a blown speaker that caused an over‑driven moment but added excitement.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mentioned in the classic Bertha duel with Jerry, providing solid rhythm guitar support.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Scat shines during the Minglewood solo; occasional B3 piano on I Know You Rider noted; overall good mix.
band_member_comments.Drums: Billy and Mickey (Rhythm Devils) receive compliments for tight, driving drumming that fuels the set.
